verdict.answer_first: A states the core answer immediately: "structuring tables so that each fact lives in exactly one place" and quickly follows with when to denormalize; B also answers upfront but adds more table/detail before guidance.
verdict.decisiveness: A gives a clear default: "normalize to 3NF first, then denormalize only where a real, measured performance problem justifies it"; B is also decisive but surveys more cases.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on right now because it gives the practical rule, common cases, and tradeoff without extra breadth that slows scanning.
